metrics.what: Thematic relevance, cultural originality, local embedding and the depth of artistic research behind the project.
metrics.scoringDiscipline:
Score inflation is a critical failure. A score of 0–2 is not a punishment — it is an accurate reflection of weak or missing evidence. Most real-world proposals do not excel in every subindex. Expect the average score across subindices to be 2–3, not 4–5. Score 3 is the baseline for a competent proposal that meets basic criteria without distinction — not a low score.

Do not round up out of sympathy, do not give benefit of the doubt, and do not infer quality from intent. If the materials do not demonstrate it, the score must reflect that absence. A strict evaluator who under-scores by 0.5 is more useful than a lenient one who over-scores by 1.

If a subindex topic is simply not present in the materials assign score 0 without hesitation. Do not fabricate relevance, do not stretch adjacent content to cover an absent topic, and do not soften the score to avoid an empty-looking result. Score 0 is the correct professional judgment when the materials provide nothing to evaluate.
ceilingRules: No evidence items at all for a subindex => score 0.
No strength=2 or 3 evidence => max 3.
<2 total evidence items => max 3, unless exactly one strength=3 => max 4.
Independence not met => max 4.
Claims-only => max 3.
Contradiction affecting subindex => score -1 and confidence max "med".

vectorRubric.0: The project materials do not address theme, originality, local connection or artistic research at all.
vectorRubric.1: Only claims — theme unarticulated, novelty asserted without basis, decorative local mention, no research layer.
vectorRubric.2: Generic theme, minor formal variation, surface-level local reference, references-only research. Mostly claims, no mechanisms.
vectorRubric.3: Clear theme with some context linkage, some original elements, local partners or place present, research actions mentioned. Competent baseline; mechanisms weak, evidence thin.
vectorRubric.4: Strong theme → context/audience chain, clear differentiator, real involvement mechanisms with local actors, method + sources present. Missing artefacts or full proof.
vectorRubric.5: Full theme → context → audience chain + culturally meaningful novelty + meaningful co-authorship with partners + structured research (method, sources, steps, artefacts). Multiple independent strength-3 items.

rubric.3: clear theme + some linkage; limited anchors
rubric.4: strong linkage + (>=1 strength=2) OR (two solid contextual anchors); missing sources/data or full problem→audience chain
rubric.5: clear theme + strong context/problem/audience chain + artifacts/sources/institutional anchors

rubric.2: minor variation; weak differentiator; mostly claims
rubric.3: some original elements; weak integration or weak evidence
rubric.4: clear differentiator + integration; lacks mechanism/artifact proof or cultural significance articulation
rubric.5: culturally meaningful novelty + (>=1 strength=3) OR (two strength=2)

rubric.2: local mention + weak involvement; unclear power; mostly claims
rubric.4: clear involvement mechanisms; weak proof or unclear co-authorship power
rubric.5: meaningful local partners/co-authors + concrete mechanisms + (strength=3 OR two strength=2)

rubric.4: method + sources present; lacks artifacts or influence trace on artistic decisions
rubric.5: structured research (question/method/sources/steps/artifacts) + explicit influence on artistic decisions + (strength=3 OR two strength=2)

metrics.what: Economic sustainability, job creation, value-chain integration and financial innovation.
metrics.scoringDiscipline:
Score inflation is a critical failure. A score of 0–2 is not a punishment — it is an accurate reflection of weak or missing evidence. Most real-world proposals do not excel in every subindex. Expect the average score across subindices to be 2–3, not 4–5. Score 3 is the baseline for a competent proposal that meets basic criteria without distinction — not a low score.

If a subindex topic is simply not present in the materials assign score 0 without hesitation. Do not fabricate relevance, do not stretch adjacent content to cover an absent topic, and do not soften the score to avoid an empty-looking result. ceilingRules: No evidence items at all for a subindex => score 0.
No strength=2 or 3 evidence => max 3.
<2 total evidence items => max 3, unless exactly one strength=3 => max 4.
Independence not met => max 4.
Claims-only => max 3.
Contradiction affecting subindex => score -1 and confidence max "med".

vectorRubric.0: No financial logic, employment dimension, market links or funding mechanism in the materials.
vectorRubric.1: Only 'will be sustainable' claims, no jobs, no market/distribution, pure grant dependence with no structure.
vectorRubric.2: Basic revenue idea, vague jobs claims, generic 'market/partners' language, minor financing variations. No numbers, no mechanisms.
vectorRubric.3: Coherent model with some numbers, named roles and channels, one described novel mechanism. Competent baseline; cost assumptions, commitments and proof are gap-y.
vectorRubric.4: Credible economics, clear job-creation plan, concrete distribution mechanics, real innovative financing — each with at least one mechanism-level evidence item. Validation/artefacts still missing.
vectorRubric.5: Validated economics + confirmed paid staffing + confirmed market/distribution access + proven or contracted innovative financing. Budgets, contracts, partner letters or prior traction present.

subindices.name: Economic sustainability
subindices.description: revenue model, diversified income, cost logic, runway

rubric.1: no financial logic; vague "will be sustainable"
rubric.2: basic revenue idea; missing costs/pricing; mostly claims
rubric.3: coherent model with some numbers or structure; gaps in costs/assumptions
rubric.4: credible model + (>=1 strength=2) OR (two solid numeric anchors); missing validation/commitments
rubric.5: validated economics + artifacts/commitments (>=1 strength=3 OR two strength=2) + clear cost/revenue chain

subindices.name: Jobs/Employment potential
subindices.description: jobs created, paid roles, meaningful work opportunities

rubric.3: roles described or some hiring plan; weak detail/commitments
rubric.4: clear job creation mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); missing proof or scale certainty
rubric.5: confirmed paid roles/partners or funded staffing plan (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + credible scale and timing

subindices.name: Integration into creative value chain
subindices.description: distribution, platforms, market links, sales channels

rubric.3: channels/platforms named; weak mechanics or proof
rubric.4: concrete go-to-market/distribution mechanics + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof or traction
rubric.5: confirmed distribution/market access (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + clear conversion path

subindices.name: Financial innovation
subindices.description: novel funding models, partnerships, financing mechanisms

rubric.1: none; standard grant dependence with no structure
rubric.3: some novel mechanism described; limited detail or evidence
rubric.4: clear innovative financing/partnership mechanism + (>=1 strength=2); weak proof
rubric.5: proven/contracted innovative financing (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + credible governance of flows

vectorRubric.0: No inclusion, accessibility, community-engagement or cultural-diversity dimension in the materials.
vectorRubric.1: Generic inclusion talk, no access provisions, audience is passive, single-language with no diversity design.
vectorRubric.2: Target groups named but without agency; 'open to all' language; 'we will engage community' claims; diversity mentioned without implementation.
vectorRubric.3: Clear target groups with some design choices, some accessibility measures, participatory activities listed, some multilingual or cultural elements. Proof limited, governance unclear.
vectorRubric.4: Strong inclusion design, defined accessibility mechanisms, real co-creation structures, deliberate multilingual/multicultural design — each with mechanism-level evidence. Delivery proof thin.
vectorRubric.5: Representation with real agency + verified accessibility across physical/economic/digital + community co-authorship with partners + delivered translation/multilingual outputs. Strength-3 artefacts.

subindices.name: Representation of marginalized voices
subindices.description: target groups, agency, voice, not tokenism

rubric.1: no target groups; generic inclusion talk
rubric.2: groups mentioned; unclear agency/mechanism; mostly claims
rubric.3: clear target groups + some design choices; limited proof
rubric.4: strong inclusion design + (>=1 strength=2) OR (two concrete anchors); weak proof of delivery/agency power
rubric.5: meaningful representation with agency + artifacts/partners (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + safeguards against tokenism

rubric.3: some access measures (pricing, ramps, online access) but incomplete
rubric.4: clear accessibility mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); gaps in verification or coverage
rubric.5: verified accessibility (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + coverage across physical/economic/digital as relevant

rubric.1: audience passive only; no engagement layer
rubric.4: defined co-creation/engagement mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: community co-authorship + concrete mechanisms + artifacts/partners (strength=3 OR two strength=2)

subindices.name: Multilingualism and multiculturality
subindices.description: languages used, cultural codes, inclusion design

rubric.3: some languages/cultural codes present; weak mechanisms
rubric.4: clear multilingual/multicultural design + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof of deliverables
rubric.5: delivered translation/multilingual outputs or contracted providers (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + coherent cultural mediation

vectorRubric.0: No concept, team information, contemporary forms or critical dimension in the materials.
vectorRubric.1: Concept unclear or absent, team unknown, only conventional forms, no critical/discursive layer.
vectorRubric.2: Basic concept stated, team names listed without roles, digital/performance mentioned in passing, generic 'raises awareness' claims.
vectorRubric.3: Clear concept with partial curatorial logic, bios and roles present, some contemporary forms described, clear themes with some discourse plan. Anchors and execution evidence limited.
vectorRubric.4: Strong concept + curatorial logic, credible roles and experience, contemporary forms with production mechanisms, defined discourse triggers and public programme. Proof limited.
vectorRubric.5: Concept excellence with realised outputs + verified team track record + prototypes/confirmed tech or venues + evidenced critical engagement (prior debate coverage, confirmed panels, commissioned texts).

rubric.2: basic idea; weak articulation; mostly claims
rubric.3: clear concept; partial curatorial logic; limited anchors
rubric.4: strong concept + curatorial logic + (>=1 strength=2) OR (two strong anchors); limited proof of execution
rubric.5: concept excellence + evidence of realized outputs/production readiness (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + coherent artistic rationale

rubric.3: some bios/roles; weak proof or uneven capacity
rubric.4: clear roles + credible experience + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof/commitments
rubric.5: strong verified track record/commitments (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + team covers key competencies

rubric.3: some contemporary forms described; weak integration or detail
rubric.4: clear contemporary forms with production mechanisms (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: strong contemporary execution evidenced by prototypes/confirmed tech/venues (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + coherent integration

rubric.3: clear questions/themes; some audience discourse plan
rubric.4: defined discourse triggers + public program/mediation mechanisms (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: evidenced critical engagement (prior debate coverage, confirmed panels, curatorial essays, commissioned texts) (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + clear theory-to-public chain

vectorRubric.0: No heritage layer, no site-specific logic and no environmental considerations in the materials.
vectorRubric.1: No heritage work, generic venue (not place-based), no sustainability consideration.
vectorRubric.2: Heritage referenced without method, place mentioned decoratively, generic 'eco-friendly' claims.
vectorRubric.3: Heritage elements present, some site-specific logic, some environmental measures listed. Methods weak, verification absent.
vectorRubric.4: Clear heritage mechanisms + site-specific concept with real mechanisms + defined sustainability mechanisms, each with at least one mechanism-level item. Proof and permissions thin.
vectorRubric.5: Structured heritage engagement with artefacts and partners + strong site integration (with permissions) + verified environmental approach with measurable practices across key footprint drivers.

rubric.4: clear heritage work mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof/outputs
rubric.5: structured heritage engagement + artifacts/partners (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + evidence of respectful interpretation

subindices.name: Place meaningfulness
subindices.description: site-specific link to history/identity of location

rubric.4: clear site-specific concept + mechanisms (>=1 strength=2); limited proof/permissions
rubric.5: strong site integration + artifacts/permissions/partners (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + explicit influence on artistic decisions

rubric.3: some measures listed; incomplete and unverified
rubric.4: defined sustainability mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited verification/coverage
rubric.5: verified environmental approach (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + measurable practices across key footprint drivers

vectorRubric.0: The project has no digital layer at all.
vectorRubric.1: No digital outputs, no technological novelty, no real access path, content is entirely static.
vectorRubric.2: Generic website/social mention, novelty claimed without method, 'online access' stated vaguely, interactivity claimed without implementation.
vectorRubric.3: Defined digital outputs, some innovative elements, some digital channels described, some interactive/data features listed. Detail partial, validation weak.
vectorRubric.4: Clear digital deliverables + innovation mechanisms (R&D steps, experimentation) + defined access mechanics (platforms, UX, onboarding) + clear interactive/data mechanisms — each with mechanism-level evidence.
vectorRubric.5: Evidenced digital delivery + validated innovation with prototypes/demos + verified low-friction access + working interactive/data components with meaningful user loops. Demos, analytics or documented R&D present.

rubric.4: clear digital deliverables + mechanisms (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: evidenced digital delivery (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + coherent documentation/distribution plan

subindices.name: Technological innovation
subindices.description: use of new tech, R&D, novel digital formats

rubric.4: clear innovation mechanisms (R&D steps, experimentation) + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: validated innovation with prototypes/demos or documented R&D outputs (strength=3 OR two strength=2)

rubric.3: some channels described; gaps in ease/availability
rubric.4: defined access mechanics (platforms, UX, onboarding) + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: verified accessibility (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + low-friction access across target audiences

subindices.name: Data/interactivity
subindices.description: user interaction, data-driven components, participation loops

rubric.3: some interactive/data features described; limited detail
rubric.4: clear interactive/data mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: working interactive/data components evidenced (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + meaningful user loop

vectorRubric.0: No international layer in the materials.
vectorRubric.1: No international partners, mono-cultural, no exchange (only export/marketing), project is site-locked.
vectorRubric.2: Generic 'international' claims, diversity asserted, 'exchange' claimed, 'scalable' asserted — all without specifics.
vectorRubric.3: Named partners, multiple cultures present in programme, some interaction formats described, some replication thinking. Roles, depth and packaging weak.
vectorRubric.4: Concrete partner mechanisms + clear multi-cultural design + defined exchange mechanisms (co-creation, residencies, dialogue) + modular replication design — each with mechanism-level evidence.
vectorRubric.5: Confirmed international partners/commitments + evidenced non-tokenistic representation + deep exchange with co-production structures + evidenced transferability (pilots, toolkits, contracted hosts).

subindices.name: International partners
subindices.description: partners from other countries, formal ties

rubric.4: concrete partner mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: confirmed international partners/commitments (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + clear roles

rubric.4: clear multi-cultural representation design + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof of delivery
rubric.5: evidenced representation (program lineup, confirmed participants) (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + non-tokenistic integration

subindices.name: Cross-cultural exchange potential
subindices.description: depth of interaction, not just presence

rubric.4: defined exchange mechanisms (co-creation, residencies, dialogue) + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: deep exchange evidenced by co-production structures/outputs (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + reciprocal benefits clear in materials

rubric.4: clear modular replication design + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof/pilots
rubric.5: evidenced transferability (pilots, replication toolkit, contracted hosts) (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + minimal dependence on unique local constraints

vectorRubric.0: No learning, skills, institutional-tie or continuation dimension in the materials.
vectorRubric.1: No education component, no skills stated, no institutional ties, no continuation plan.
vectorRubric.2: Vague 'learning' mention, generic 'skills' claim, generic 'collaboration' statement, 'community will remain' claim. No structure.
vectorRubric.3: Some workshops described, specific skills named, institutions named, some continuation elements. Curricula, methods and governance are weak.
vectorRubric.4: Structured educational mechanisms + clear skill-building with outputs + defined partnership mechanisms + defined post-project mechanisms (platform, governance, funding) — each with mechanism-level evidence.
vectorRubric.5: Evidenced education delivery with partners + evidenced skill transfer (portfolios, certifications, partner validation) + confirmed long-term institutional agreements + evidenced sustainability infrastructure.

rubric.3: some workshops described; weak curriculum detail
rubric.4: structured educational mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: evidenced education delivery/partners (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + coherent learning design

rubric.4: clear skill-building mechanism + outputs + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: evidenced skill transfer (artifacts, portfolios, certifications, partner validation) (strength=3 OR two strength=2)

rubric.4: defined partnership mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: confirmed long-term institutional agreements (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + roles and continuity explicit

rubric.4: defined post-project mechanisms (community, platform, governance, funding) + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: evidenced sustainability infrastructure (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + credible continuity without constant new grants

vectorRubric.0: No participation or dialogue dimension at all.
vectorRubric.1: Audience is passive only, no dialogue, process opaque, one-size-fits-all with exclusion risks ignored.
vectorRubric.2: Engagement claimed, generic 'discussion' claim, vague process mention, 'inclusive' claim — all without design.
vectorRubric.3: Some participatory formats + dialogue formats listed + some stages described + some adaptations for different audiences. Depth weak, detail incomplete.
vectorRubric.4: Clear engagement mechanisms + dialogue/feedback mechanisms + transparency mechanisms (roadmap, decision logic) + inclusive participation design — each with mechanism-level evidence.
vectorRubric.5: Evidenced high-engagement formats + evidenced dialogue depth (documented feedback use, published outputs) + evidenced transparency (public timeline, governance, open updates) + evidenced inclusivity (access adaptations, multi-mode).

rubric.4: clear engagement mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: evidenced high engagement formats and readiness (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + credible facilitation capacity

rubric.3: dialogue formats listed; weak depth/feedback structure
rubric.4: clear dialogue/feedback mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: evidenced dialogue depth (curated discussions, documented feedback use, published outputs) (strength=3 OR two strength=2)

rubric.4: clear transparency mechanisms (roadmap, decision logic, open comms) + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: evidenced transparency (published timeline, governance, open updates) (strength=3 OR two strength=2)

rubric.4: clear inclusive participation design + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: evidenced inclusivity (access adaptations, multiple modes, facilitation for diverse groups) (strength=3 OR two strength=2)

vectorRubric.0: No resonance dimension (media, reputation, risk, continuation) in the materials.
vectorRubric.1: No media angle, no reputational signalling, risk ignored or naive provocation, strictly one-off.
vectorRubric.2: 'We will promote' claims, vague prestige language, risk acknowledged vaguely, vague continuation ideas.
vectorRubric.3: Some media packaging, some institutional or brand signals, risk noted with partial mitigation, some follow-up ideas. Plans weak, proof thin.
vectorRubric.4: Clear media mechanisms + reputational mechanisms (endorsements, positioning) + explicit risk assessment with mitigation + defined continuation mechanisms — each with mechanism-level evidence.
vectorRubric.5: Evidenced media traction or committed partners/assets + evidenced status lift via institutions/prior recognition + evidenced preparedness (protocols, moderation plans, stakeholder mapping) + evidenced continuation commitments (venues, productised outputs).

rubric.4: clear media mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof/partners
rubric.5: evidenced media traction or committed partners/assets (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + strong narrative hook

subindices.name: Reputational value
subindices.description: status lift for organizers/artists, institutional signaling

rubric.4: clear reputational mechanisms (partners, endorsements, positioning) + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof
rubric.5: evidenced status lift via institutions/partners or prior recognition in materials (strength=3 OR two strength=2)

rubric.4: clear risk assessment + mitigation mechanisms (>=1 strength=2); limited proof of preparedness
rubric.5: evidenced preparedness (protocols, stakeholder mapping, moderation plan) (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + clear boundaries and intent

subindices.name: Post-effect/continuation potential
subindices.description: spin-offs, follow-up formats, scalability of impact

rubric.4: defined continuation mechanisms + (>=1 strength=2); limited proof/commitments
rubric.5: evidenced continuation commitments (venues, partners, productized outputs) (strength=3 OR two strength=2) + plausible sustained trajectory
